title: Native Tracker Asset
description:
A single renderer-fired tracker URL bound to a native creative event (impression, viewability, click, custom). Native parity to `vast_tracker` — buyer ships the URL, the native renderer fires it at serve time. The buyer's measurement vendor declares the tracker URL; the seller's native renderer fires it without buyer-side involvement at impression time.

**Scope boundary (normative).** `native_tracker` covers RENDERER-FIRED trackers — measurement events that the native ad's serving template invokes when the user sees, views, or clicks the creative. Conversion pixels that fire on the advertiser's site after the click (Meta Pixel, GA4 server-side, custom postbacks) MUST be modeled via `sync_event_sources` / `event_log` — they are campaign-scoped, not creative-asset-scoped. See `docs/creative/canonical-formats.mdx#what-format_kind-is-not-for`.

Maps to IAB OpenRTB Native 1.2 / Dynamic Native Ads API tracker objects:
- `imptrackers[]` → one `native_tracker` per entry with `event: impression`, `method: img`
- `jstracker` → one `native_tracker` with `event: impression`, `method: js`
- `eventtrackers[]` → one `native_tracker` per entry, `event` mapped from the IAB event enum
- `link.clicktrackers[]` → one `native_tracker` per entry with `event: click`, `method: img` (click destinations live on `landing_page_url` slot)

asset_type.description: Discriminator identifying this as a native tracker asset. See /schemas/creative/asset-types for the registry.

event.description:
Which native event this tracker fires on. Mirrors IAB OpenRTB Native 1.2 event-tracker enum:
- `impression` — fires when the ad is served (covers both `imptrackers[]` and `jstracker` from the IAB shape — distinguished by `method`)
- `viewable_mrc_50` — IAB MRC viewable, 50% pixels for ≥1 second
- `viewable_mrc_100` — IAB MRC viewable, 100% pixels for ≥1 second
- `viewable_video_50` — video-specific viewable, 50% pixels for ≥2 seconds with audio on
- `click` — fires when the user clicks the creative (`link.clicktrackers[]`)
- `custom` — adopter-defined event; MUST also set `custom_event_name`

method.description: How the tracker URL is invoked at serve time: - `img` — fired as an image pixel (HTTP GET with ``-like semantics; no JS execution) - `js` — fired as a script include (renderer evaluates the URL's response as JavaScript) Matches IAB OpenRTB Native 1.2 method enum (1=img, 2=js). `js` MUST only be used by sellers whose native renderer supports JavaScript trackers; sellers without JS-tracker support MUST reject `method: js` declarations at sync_creatives time with `CREATIVE_REJECTED` carrying the reason.

url.description: Tracker URL fired when `event` occurs. May carry AdCP universal macros (e.g., `{MEDIA_BUY_ID}`, `{CREATIVE_ID}`, `{CACHEBUSTER}`); the seller's native renderer URL-encodes substituted values at serve time. See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx.

custom_event_name.description: REQUIRED when `event` is `custom`; otherwise MUST be absent. Adopter-defined event name. Sellers without registered handling for a given custom_event_name MUST silently no-op (do not fire) rather than reject — custom events are forward-compatible probes.

provenance.description: Provenance metadata for this asset, overrides manifest-level provenance.
